official confirmed, leading a United Nations agency to pause an evacuation effort

items.title: UN agency pauses evacuation of ships through the Strait of Hormuz after attack on vessel
items.content: (Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi/AP Photo/Amirhosein Khorgooi)

items.title: Oil prices ease after spiking over halt to Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan
items.standfirst: Brent crude subsides after attack in waterway prompted jump in prices.
items.content: Brent crude subsides after attack in waterway prompted jump in prices.

items.title: Iran strikes ship in Strait of Hormuz in challenge to U.S.-Iran deal
items.standfirst: Iran attacked a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, a U.S. official confirmed, leading a United Nations agency to pause an evacuation effort.
items.content: Iran attacked a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, a U.S. official confirmed, leading a United Nations agency to pause an evacuation effort.

items.title: US wants Iran deal but 'not at any price', Rubio says
items.standfirst: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Thursday Washington would not accept any Iran deal allowing Tehran to impose fees on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, warning it would cause "total chaos". His remarks came after an attack on a vessel forced the United Nations to suspend mariner evacuation efforts.
items.content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Thursday Washington would not accept any Iran deal allowing Tehran to impose fees on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, warning it would cause "total chaos". His remarks came after an attack on a vessel forced the United Nations to suspend mariner evacuation efforts.

items.title: UN suspends Strait of Hormuz evacuation after ship 'hit by projectile'
items.standfirst: The UN has halted the evacuation of thousands of seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel came under a suspected attack.
items.content: The UN has halted the evacuation of thousands of seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel came under a suspected attack.

items.title: U.N. agency pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation effort as ship is struck off Oman
items.standfirst: Iran warned that any ship not following its approved route in the strait “will be dealt with accordingly.”
items.content: Iran warned that any ship not following its approved route in the strait “will be dealt with accordingly.”

clusters.title: Venezuelans search through rubble as earthquake death toll rises
clusters.neutral_headline: Aerial footage reveals destruction in coastal Venezuela
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: Venezuelans search through rubble as earthquake death toll rises
items.standfirst: Massive search-and-rescue operations are underway in Venezuela Friday as civilians and emergency workers rushed to find those trapped beneath the rubble more than a day after devastating twin earthquakes killed over 230 people.
items.content: Massive search-and-rescue operations are underway in Venezuela Friday as civilians and emergency workers rushed to find those trapped beneath the rubble more than a day after devastating twin earthquakes killed over 230 people.

items.title: Race to find survivors after Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes
items.standfirst: The back-to-back earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 230 people, and the toll is likely to keep rising as rescue and recovery operations ramp up.
items.content: The back-to-back earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 230 people, and the toll is likely to keep rising as rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

items.title: Aerial footage reveals destruction in coastal Venezuela
items.standfirst: Multi-storey buildings have collapsed in La Guaira following two major earthquakes on Wednesday.
items.content: Multi-storey buildings have collapsed in La Guaira following two major earthquakes on Wednesday.

items.title: What we know so far
items.standfirst: The two quakes have killed at least 235 people and injured more than 4,300, with more feared trapped under rubble.
items.content: The two quakes have killed at least 235 people and injured more than 4,300, with more feared trapped under rubble.

items.title: Rescuers search rubble for survivors as Venezuela earthquakes kill at least 235
items.standfirst: At least 4,300 people have been injured after two seven-plus magnitude shallow quakes hit seconds apart.
items.content: At least 4,300 people have been injured after two seven-plus magnitude shallow quakes hit seconds apart.

items.title: Venezuela quake survivors describe devastation: "Everything collapsed"
items.standfirst: A Caracas resident told CBS News that he "started to pray" when he felt the first earthquake hit Venezuela.
items.content: A Caracas resident told CBS News that he "started to pray" when he felt the first earthquake hit Venezuela.

items.title: Hundreds trapped beneath buildings a day after Venezuela’s twin earthquakes
items.standfirst: Emergency crews are racing to rescue hundreds trapped under collapsed buildings after earthquakes struck Venezuela.
items.content: Emergency crews are racing to rescue hundreds trapped under collapsed buildings after earthquakes struck Venezuela.

items.title: Rescues and prayers a day after Venezuelan earthquakes
items.standfirst: The BBC's Vanessa Silva reports from Caracas as the city faces the aftermath of back-to-back tremors.
items.content: The BBC's Vanessa Silva reports from Caracas as the city faces the aftermath of back-to-back tremors.

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes live: Death toll rises to at least 235
items.standfirst: The death toll from two back-to-back earthquakes in Venezuela has risen to about 235, with at least 4,300 people injured, Health Minister Carlos Alvarado said on state television on Thursday as rescue teams continued searching for survivors. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: The death toll from two back-to-back earthquakes in Venezuela has risen to about 235, with at least 4,300 people injured, Health Minister Carlos Alvarado said on state television on Thursday as rescue teams continued searching for survivors. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Venezuelan airport outside Caracas reopens after back-to-back earthquakes
items.standfirst: People in northern Venezuela are racing against time to pull survivors from the rubble of collapsed buildings after a pair of powerful earthquakes hit the country on Wednesday. CBS News correspondent Cristian Benavides has the latest from Bogotá, Colombia. Then, CBS News meteorologist Darren Peck joins to examine the risk of aftershocks.
items.content: People in northern Venezuela are racing against time to pull survivors from the rubble of collapsed buildings after a pair of powerful earthquakes hit the country on Wednesday. CBS News correspondent Cristian Benavides has the latest from Bogotá, Colombia. Then, CBS News meteorologist Darren Peck joins to examine the risk of aftershocks.

items.title: Search for survivors underway as earthquake in Venezuela puts strain on resources
items.standfirst: Back-to-back earthquakes hit Venezuela, killing at least 188 people and injuring thousands. NBC News' Ana Vanessa Herrero reports from Caracas about the search for survivors.
items.content: Back-to-back earthquakes hit Venezuela, killing at least 188 people and injuring thousands. NBC News' Ana Vanessa Herrero reports from Caracas about the search for survivors.

items.title: NBC News reporter in Venezuela says people she knows might be missing under rubble
items.standfirst: Ana Vanessa Herrero, reporting for NBC News in Venezuela, sheds light on the hundreds of people looking for loved ones who might be missing under the rubble after two major earthquakes hit near the country's capital on Wednesday.
items.content: Ana Vanessa Herrero, reporting for NBC News in Venezuela, sheds light on the hundreds of people looking for loved ones who might be missing under the rubble after two major earthquakes hit near the country's capital on Wednesday.

items.title: 7.1-magnitude earthquake rattles Venezuela
items.standfirst: Buildings collapsed in Caracas, Venezuela, after a powerful 7.1-magnitude earthquake hit along the country’s Caribbean coast. NBC News’ Aaron Gilchrist reports.
items.content: Buildings collapsed in Caracas, Venezuela, after a powerful 7.1-magnitude earthquake hit along the country’s Caribbean coast. NBC News’ Aaron Gilchrist reports.

items.title: Deadly quakes are among the strongest to strike Venezuela in more than a century
items.standfirst: Two powerful earthquake shook Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas.
items.content: Two powerful earthquake shook Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas.

clusters.title: US ends deportation protections (TPS) for Haitians and Syrians
clusters.neutral_headline: US ends deportation protections (TPS) for Haitians and Syrians
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, BBC World, CBS News

items.title: US ends deportation protections (TPS) for Haitians and Syrians
items.standfirst: The US Supreme Court has sided with the Trump administration in its bid to end Temporary Protected Status (TPS).
items.content: The US Supreme Court has sided with the Trump administration in its bid to end Temporary Protected Status (TPS).

items.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
items.standfirst: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.
items.content: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

items.title: Takeaways from Supreme Court's 2 immigration opinions
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court issued two major immigration decisions Thursday, including one that lifts deportation restrictions for thousands of Haitian and Syrian immigrants. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks them down.
items.content: The Supreme Court issued two major immigration decisions Thursday, including one that lifts deportation restrictions for thousands of Haitian and Syrian immigrants. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks them down.

items.title: Cambridge hospital staff investigated over accessing records of boy hurt in crocodile pit
items.standfirst: About 40 members of staff reported to have looked at information of boy, three, who ended up in zoo enclosureAbout 40 members of hospital staff accessed the medical records of a three-year-old boy hurt in a crocodile pit, prompting an investigation, it has been reported.Cambridge University Hospitals (CUH) has referred itself to the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) and is investigating whether all the workers had a legitimate reason for looking at his information. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
clusters.neutral_headline: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, NPR News

items.title: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
items.standfirst: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.
items.content: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.

items.title: Reflecting Pool liner was cut with a sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
items.standfirst: A top official at the National Park Service says a liner along the bottom of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool was cut with a sharp knife or razor this month, causing damage to the foam sealant installed as part of a $16 million rehabilitation project.
items.content: A top official at the National Park Service says a liner along the bottom of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool was cut with a sharp knife or razor this month, causing damage to the foam sealant installed as part of a $16 million rehabilitation project.(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)

clusters.title: Venezuela’s Powerful Earthquakes Were a Rare ‘Seismic Doublet’
clusters.neutral_headline: Venezuela’s Powerful Earthquakes Were a Rare ‘Seismic Doublet’

items.title: Venezuela’s Powerful Earthquakes Were a Rare ‘Seismic Doublet’
items.standfirst: The country was hit hard by a pair of quakes that happened in quick succession and were likely driven by stress being transferred from one part of the fault that runs through the country to another.
items.content: The country was hit hard by a pair of quakes that happened in quick succession and were likely driven by stress being transferred from one part of the fault that runs through the country to another.

items.title: What to know about ‘doublet’ earthquakes like the destructive pair that hit Venezuela
items.standfirst: Venezuela’s pair of earthquakes is considered a doublet sequence. It’s not rare to have two quakes in short succession, but in this case, that most likely led to more destruction.
items.content: Venezuela’s pair of earthquakes is considered a doublet sequence. It’s not rare to have two quakes in short succession, but in this case, that most likely led to more destruction.
